evals.name: idempotency
evals.prompt: Scaffold a groundwork plan folder at ./plans/eval-share-card/ with profile software and goal "Lift the share-card renderer into its own pkg". Use the fast path (all inputs are given, no interview needed). Then run groundwork init a SECOND time on the exact same folder. Report what the second run did.
evals.expected_output: First run scaffolds the standard spine (00-README, 01-plan, 02/03-research, 04-discussion, 05-tracking, .groundwork.json, designs/, drafts/). .groundwork.json is valid JSON with profile=software, spine_version=1, the goal, and a docs map. Second run is a NO-OP: it reports no changes; spine files are byte-identical to after the first run.
assertions: .groundwork.json is valid JSON declaring profile=software and spine_version=1
No unrendered {{...}} placeholders remain in scaffolded files
Reports the second scaffold run as a no-op / byte-identical (idempotent)

evals.name: discover-triggering
evals.prompt: I'm about to start a real piece of work: migrating our marketing site's blog from Webflow over to Payload CMS. It's going to span a few weeks, needs a research pass, a couple of architecture decisions, and several workstreams that can run in parallel. Set me up a proper plan folder for it under ./plans/ so I can actually track this.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this as non-trivial multi-doc planning work and uses groundwork init (discover path). Creates a plan folder under ./plans/ with the full spine + a valid .groundwork.json. Picks a sensible profile (software is defensible since it's a CMS migration; general is also acceptable) and a coherent one-sentence goal. Does NOT just write a single markdown file.
assertions: Creates a multi-document plan folder (not a single markdown file)
Produces the canonical groundwork spine + a valid .groundwork.json anchor
Chooses a coherent profile and a one-sentence goal recorded in the anchor

evals.name: subplan-clobber-safety
evals.prompt: First set up a fixture, then do the real task. FIXTURE: create a groundwork plan folder at ./existing-plan/ using the fast path (profile software, goal "Stabilize the public API gateway", no interview). Then plant four hand-written paragraphs as MY notes — each on its own line, OUTSIDE any groundwork:auto fence — containing exactly these literal markers: HANDWRITTEN-SENTINEL-1 in 00-README.md, HANDWRITTEN-SENTINEL-2 and HANDWRITTEN-SENTINEL-3 in 01-plan.md, and HANDWRITTEN-SENTINEL-4 in 05-tracking.md. TASK: now add a decision-doc sub-plan to ./existing-plan/ capturing the choice of switching the gateway from REST to GraphQL. Make sure you don't disturb any of my hand-written notes in that folder.
evals.expected_output: First seeds ./existing-plan/ as a valid groundwork folder with four hand-written HANDWRITTEN-SENTINEL-{1..4} paragraphs placed outside fences across 00-README.md (1), 01-plan.md (2 and 3), and 05-tracking.md (4). Then creates a new NN-*.md sub-plan (number >= 06) from the decision-doc archetype, registers it in .groundwork.json.subplans, and cross-links it from 00-README's spine-index fence. Critically: all four HANDWRITTEN-SENTINEL-* paragraphs survive byte-for-byte. .groundwork.json remains valid JSON.
assertions: Creates a new NN-*.md sub-plan numbered >= 06
All four HANDWRITTEN-SENTINEL-* markers survive across 00/01/05
Cross-links the sub-plan from inside the 00-README spine-index fence

evals.name: emit-workflow
evals.prompt: Scaffold a groundwork plan at ./plans/eval-emit/ (profile software, goal "Add a share-card export endpoint"). Populate 05-tracking.md with two work packages: WP-01 (freezes a shared contract, owns a freeze gate G-CONTRACT) and WP-02 (depends on WP-01). Register the IDs. Then run groundwork orchestrate with the --emit-workflow flag. Show me what got written.
evals.expected_output: Runs orchestrate (clarify pre-step, derives the wave plan), writes 09-orchestration.md AND emits artifact/orchestrate.workflow.js — a runnable Workflow script. The script begins with `export const meta`, declares one phase per wave, fans out the wave's WPs with parallel(), runs a VERDICT-schema adversarial verify at the G-CONTRACT freeze-gate barrier, and sets a model tier per WP. orchestrate offers to run it rather than auto-running. The prose 09-orchestration.md still exists as the human-readable twin.
assertions: Writes artifact/orchestrate.workflow.js whose first statement is `export const meta`
The workflow has one meta.phases entry per wave and fans out WPs with parallel()
Includes a freeze-gate barrier that adversarially verifies WP reports (VERDICT schema) before crossing
Still writes the prose 09-orchestration.md (the executable script is an additive twin, not a replacement)
Offers to run the workflow rather than auto-running it (opt-in, explicit confirm)
